sourceCollection.name: Englishphile Pilot Database Pack 001 - All In One
sourceCollection.description: Optional combined original pilot content. Import either this file OR the individual packs, not both.
sourceCollection.copyrightNote: Original Englishphile pilot content generated for internal educational use; not copied verbatim from uploaded worksheets or third-party books.

problems.title: Advanced MCQ Set 01
problems.statement: Chọn đáp án đúng nhất để hoàn thành câu.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, idiom, inversion, grammar nâng cao và vocabulary sắc thái.

questions.prompt: The committee decided to _____ the proposal until more evidence was available.
Her explanation was so _____ that even the weakest students understood the concept.
The new policy has come in for fierce _____ from parents and teachers.
He is not lazy; if anything, he is _____ to overwork himself.
The witness statement was at _____ with the video evidence.

problems.title: Advanced MCQ Set 02
problems.statement: Chọn đáp án đúng nhất để hoàn thành câu.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, idiom, inversion, grammar nâng cao và vocabulary sắc thái.

questions.prompt: We must take his age into _____ before judging his behaviour.
No sooner had the bell rung _____ the students rushed out.
The manager insisted that the report _____ by Friday.
The plan sounds promising, but it may be difficult to put into _____.
She has a remarkable ability to _____ complex ideas into simple diagrams.

problems.title: Advanced MCQ Set 03
problems.statement: Chọn đáp án đúng nhất để hoàn thành câu.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, idiom, inversion, grammar nâng cao và vocabulary sắc thái.

questions.prompt: The essay was good in content, but it lacked _____ between paragraphs.
The company is trying to _____ a new image after years of bad publicity.
Hardly _____ entered the room when everyone fell silent.
The article makes a _____ case for banning single-use plastic.
The team worked against the _____ to finish before midnight.

problems.title: Advanced MCQ Set 04
problems.statement: Chọn đáp án đúng nhất để hoàn thành câu.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, idiom, inversion, grammar nâng cao và vocabulary sắc thái.

questions.prompt: I was under the _____ that the meeting had been cancelled.
His comments were intended to _____ debate, not to offend anyone.
She is very good at thinking on her _____.
The novelist’s style is difficult to _____.
The results should be treated with _____ because the sample was small.

problems.title: Advanced MCQ Set 05
problems.statement: Chọn đáp án đúng nhất để hoàn thành câu.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, idiom, inversion, grammar nâng cao và vocabulary sắc thái.

questions.prompt: Only after several failed attempts _____ the correct method.
The two accounts do not _____ with each other.
He tried to _____ responsibility for the mistake onto his assistant.
The speech was full of _____ phrases but short on practical solutions.
The committee reached a decision by a narrow _____.

problems.title: Advanced MCQ Set 06
problems.statement: Chọn đáp án đúng nhất để hoàn thành câu.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, idiom, inversion, grammar nâng cao và vocabulary sắc thái.

questions.prompt: She takes great _____ in her students progress.
The director refused to _____ on the details of the new project.
The plan was rejected on the _____ that it was too expensive.
I have no _____ of changing my mind.
The evidence is not strong enough to _____ such a serious accusation.

problems.title: Advanced MCQ Set 07
problems.statement: Chọn đáp án đúng nhất để hoàn thành câu.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, idiom, inversion, grammar nâng cao và vocabulary sắc thái.

questions.prompt: It is high time the school _____ its outdated policy.
His behaviour left a lot to be _____.
questions.explanation: leave a lot to be desired.

questions.prompt: The charity relies heavily _____ donations from the public.
The witness was reluctant to _____ further details.
Her calmness under pressure _____ her apart from other candidates.

problems.title: Advanced MCQ Set 08
problems.statement: Chọn đáp án đúng nhất để hoàn thành câu.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, idiom, inversion, grammar nâng cao và vocabulary sắc thái.

questions.prompt: The argument was based on a _____ assumption.
The speaker’s point was lost _____ the noise at the back.
His apology did little to _____ the damage caused by his remarks.
The government has pledged to _____ down on illegal dumping.
She was _____ to tears by the final scene.

problems.title: Sentence Transformation Set 01
problems.statement: Viết lại câu sao cho nghĩa không đổi.
problems.instructions: Nếu có từ khóa, không thay đổi dạng của từ khóa. Hệ thống chỉ chấm đúng tuyệt đối; câu khác đáp án mẫu sẽ cần giáo viên kiểm tra.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: I only realised how serious the problem was when I read the report.

accepted: Not until I read the report did I realise how serious the problem was.
answer.display: Not until I read the report did I realise how serious the problem was.

metadata.originalSentence: I only realised how serious the problem was when I read the report.
metadata.modelAnswer: Not until I read the report did I realise how serious the problem was.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: She was so absorbed in the book that she missed her stop.

accepted: She was so wrapped up in the book that she missed her stop.
answer.display: She was so wrapped up in the book that she missed her stop.

metadata.originalSentence: She was so absorbed in the book that she missed her stop.
metadata.modelAnswer: She was so wrapped up in the book that she missed her stop.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The committee will not approve the plan under any circumstances.

accepted: Under no circumstances will the committee approve the plan.
answer.display: Under no circumstances will the committee approve the plan.

metadata.originalSentence: The committee will not approve the plan under any circumstances.
metadata.modelAnswer: Under no circumstances will the committee approve the plan.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: He regrets not applying for the scholarship.

accepted: He wishes he had applied for the scholarship.
answer.display: He wishes he had applied for the scholarship.

metadata.originalSentence: He regrets not applying for the scholarship.
metadata.modelAnswer: He wishes he had applied for the scholarship.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The lecture was too technical for most students to understand.

accepted: It was such a technical lecture that most students could not understand it.
answer.display: It was such a technical lecture that most students could not understand it.

metadata.originalSentence: The lecture was too technical for most students to understand.
metadata.modelAnswer: It was such a technical lecture that most students could not understand it.

problems.title: Sentence Transformation Set 02
problems.statement: Viết lại câu sao cho nghĩa không đổi.
problems.instructions: Nếu có từ khóa, không thay đổi dạng của từ khóa. Hệ thống chỉ chấm đúng tuyệt đối; câu khác đáp án mẫu sẽ cần giáo viên kiểm tra.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: I would prefer you not to mention this to anyone.

accepted: I would rather you did not mention this to anyone.
answer.display: I would rather you did not mention this to anyone.

metadata.originalSentence: I would prefer you not to mention this to anyone.
metadata.modelAnswer: I would rather you did not mention this to anyone.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The manager did not explain the delay until the meeting ended.

accepted: Only after the meeting ended did the manager explain the delay.
answer.display: Only after the meeting ended did the manager explain the delay.

metadata.originalSentence: The manager did not explain the delay until the meeting ended.
metadata.modelAnswer: Only after the meeting ended did the manager explain the delay.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: His failure to revise thoroughly led to his poor result.

accepted: He got a poor result because he had not revised thoroughly.
answer.display: He got a poor result because he had not revised thoroughly.

metadata.originalSentence: His failure to revise thoroughly led to his poor result.
metadata.modelAnswer: He got a poor result because he had not revised thoroughly.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The film was not nearly as impressive as the reviews suggested.

accepted: The film was nowhere near as impressive as the reviews suggested.
answer.display: The film was nowhere near as impressive as the reviews suggested.

metadata.originalSentence: The film was not nearly as impressive as the reviews suggested.
metadata.modelAnswer: The film was nowhere near as impressive as the reviews suggested.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: No one expected the quiet student to win the debate.

accepted: The quiet student turned out to be the winner of the debate.
answer.display: The quiet student turned out to be the winner of the debate.

metadata.originalSentence: No one expected the quiet student to win the debate.
metadata.modelAnswer: The quiet student turned out to be the winner of the debate.

problems.title: Sentence Transformation Set 03
problems.statement: Viết lại câu sao cho nghĩa không đổi.
problems.instructions: Nếu có từ khóa, không thay đổi dạng của từ khóa. Hệ thống chỉ chấm đúng tuyệt đối; câu khác đáp án mẫu sẽ cần giáo viên kiểm tra.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: If the weather had not changed suddenly, we would have reached the summit.

accepted: But for the sudden change in the weather, we would have reached the summit.
answer.display: But for the sudden change in the weather, we would have reached the summit.

metadata.originalSentence: If the weather had not changed suddenly, we would have reached the summit.
metadata.modelAnswer: But for the sudden change in the weather, we would have reached the summit.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The teacher said that the essay needed a clearer argument.

accepted: The teacher said that the essay called for a clearer argument.
answer.display: The teacher said that the essay called for a clearer argument.

metadata.originalSentence: The teacher said that the essay needed a clearer argument.
metadata.modelAnswer: The teacher said that the essay called for a clearer argument.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: She did not lose her temper despite the unfair criticism.

accepted: She kept her temper despite the unfair criticism.
answer.display: She kept her temper despite the unfair criticism.

metadata.originalSentence: She did not lose her temper despite the unfair criticism.
metadata.modelAnswer: She kept her temper despite the unfair criticism.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: He spoke so quietly that nobody at the back could hear him.

accepted: He could not make himself heard at the back because he spoke so quietly.
answer.display: He could not make himself heard at the back because he spoke so quietly.

metadata.originalSentence: He spoke so quietly that nobody at the back could hear him.
metadata.modelAnswer: He could not make himself heard at the back because he spoke so quietly.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: They believe the painting was stolen during the night.

accepted: The painting is thought to have been stolen during the night.
answer.display: The painting is thought to have been stolen during the night.

metadata.originalSentence: They believe the painting was stolen during the night.
metadata.modelAnswer: The painting is thought to have been stolen during the night.

problems.title: Sentence Transformation Set 04
problems.statement: Viết lại câu sao cho nghĩa không đổi.
problems.instructions: Nếu có từ khóa, không thay đổi dạng của từ khóa. Hệ thống chỉ chấm đúng tuyệt đối; câu khác đáp án mẫu sẽ cần giáo viên kiểm tra.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The company had never faced such a serious crisis before.

accepted: Never before had the company faced such a serious crisis.
answer.display: Never before had the company faced such a serious crisis.

metadata.originalSentence: The company had never faced such a serious crisis before.
metadata.modelAnswer: Never before had the company faced such a serious crisis.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: It was careless of him to leave the documents on the train.

accepted: He should not have left the documents on the train.
answer.display: He should not have left the documents on the train.

metadata.originalSentence: It was careless of him to leave the documents on the train.
metadata.modelAnswer: He should not have left the documents on the train.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The new rule made many students confused.

accepted: The new rule gave rise to confusion among many students.
answer.display: The new rule gave rise to confusion among many students.

metadata.originalSentence: The new rule made many students confused.
metadata.modelAnswer: The new rule gave rise to confusion among many students.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: She immediately accepted the offer when it was made.

accepted: No sooner was the offer made than she accepted it.
answer.display: No sooner was the offer made than she accepted it.

metadata.originalSentence: She immediately accepted the offer when it was made.
metadata.modelAnswer: No sooner was the offer made than she accepted it.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: His explanation did not convince me at all.

accepted: I found his explanation completely unconvincing.
answer.display: I found his explanation completely unconvincing.

metadata.originalSentence: His explanation did not convince me at all.
metadata.modelAnswer: I found his explanation completely unconvincing.

problems.title: Sentence Transformation Set 05
problems.statement: Viết lại câu sao cho nghĩa không đổi.
problems.instructions: Nếu có từ khóa, không thay đổi dạng của từ khóa. Hệ thống chỉ chấm đúng tuyệt đối; câu khác đáp án mẫu sẽ cần giáo viên kiểm tra.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: They did not start repairing the bridge until funding was secured.

accepted: Not until funding was secured did they start repairing the bridge.
answer.display: Not until funding was secured did they start repairing the bridge.

metadata.originalSentence: They did not start repairing the bridge until funding was secured.
metadata.modelAnswer: Not until funding was secured did they start repairing the bridge.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The principal’s speech encouraged us to work harder.

accepted: The principal’s speech spurred us on to work harder.
answer.display: The principal’s speech spurred us on to work harder.

metadata.originalSentence: The principal’s speech encouraged us to work harder.
metadata.modelAnswer: The principal’s speech spurred us on to work harder.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: It is possible that she misunderstood the instructions.

accepted: She may have misunderstood the instructions.
answer.display: She may have misunderstood the instructions.

metadata.originalSentence: It is possible that she misunderstood the instructions.
metadata.modelAnswer: She may have misunderstood the instructions.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The team failed because they underestimated their opponents.

accepted: The team’s failure can be accounted for by their underestimating their opponents.
answer.display: The team’s failure can be accounted for by their underestimating their opponents.

metadata.originalSentence: The team failed because they underestimated their opponents.
metadata.modelAnswer: The team’s failure can be accounted for by their underestimating their opponents.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the sentence so that it has a similar meaning: The question was so difficult that nobody answered it correctly.

accepted: Such was the difficulty of the question that nobody answered it correctly.
answer.display: Such was the difficulty of the question that nobody answered it correctly.

metadata.originalSentence: The question was so difficult that nobody answered it correctly.
metadata.modelAnswer: Such was the difficulty of the question that nobody answered it correctly.

problems.title: Guided Cloze: The quiet power of routines
problems.statement: Đọc đoạn văn và chọn đáp án phù hợp nhất cho từng chỗ trống.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, logic đoạn văn và sắc thái nghĩa.

questions.prompt: Blank 1
questions.passage: Routines are often dismissed as dull, but they can be a powerful (0) _____ of mental energy. When repeated actions become automatic, the mind is (1) _____ to focus on decisions that genuinely matter. This is why many successful students keep a fixed study schedule. They are not trying to remove freedom from their lives; rather, they are trying to (2) _____ distractions. A routine also makes progress more (3) _____, because small actions are repeated before motivation has time to fade. Of course, a routine that is too rigid can become (4) _____.

problems.title: Guided Cloze: The value of mistakes
problems.statement: Đọc đoạn văn và chọn đáp án phù hợp nhất cho từng chỗ trống.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, logic đoạn văn và sắc thái nghĩa.

questions.prompt: Blank 1
questions.passage: Many students treat mistakes as proof of failure, but in language learning they are often signs of (0) _____. A wrong answer can reveal a hidden assumption about grammar, collocation or meaning. The important thing is not simply to correct the answer, but to (1) _____ the reason behind the mistake. Students who keep a record of recurring errors are more likely to notice patterns and (2) _____ them over time. In this sense, a mistake is not an obstacle to progress but a useful (3) _____ for improvement.

problems.statement: Đọc đoạn văn và chọn đáp án phù hợp nhất cho từng chỗ trống.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, logic đoạn văn và sắc thái nghĩa.

questions.prompt: Blank 1
questions.passage: Good readers do more than identify facts. They notice how ideas are connected, why examples are included, and what the writer may be (0) _____. Careful readers slow down at contrast markers because these words often signal a shift in (3) _____.

problems.title: Guided Cloze: Why transitions matter
problems.statement: Đọc đoạn văn và chọn đáp án phù hợp nhất cho từng chỗ trống.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào collocation, logic đoạn văn và sắc thái nghĩa.

questions.prompt: Blank 1
questions.passage: Transitions are not decorative words. They show the (0) _____ between ideas and help readers follow the writer reasoning. However, transitions must be chosen carefully. For example, therefore signals a result, whereas nevertheless introduces a contrast. Using the wrong connector can (1) _____ the logic of an argument. Strong writers vary their language while keeping the relationship between ideas (2) _____.

questions.prompt: Blank 2
questions.passage: Transitions are not decorative words. They show the (0) _____ between ideas and help readers follow the writer reasoning. However, transitions must be chosen carefully. Strong writers vary their language while keeping the relationship between ideas (2) _____.

questions.prompt: Blank 1

Without review, new knowledge can fade (2) _____ quickly than expected. A short review session the next day helps the brain strengthen connections and identify (3) _____ is still unclear. This is why effective learners return to old mistakes instead (4) _____ simply moving on to new exercises.

problems.title: Open Cloze: A balanced argument
problems.statement: Điền một từ thích hợp vào mỗi chỗ trống.
problems.instructions: Mỗi chỗ trống chỉ điền một từ. Chú ý liên từ, giới từ, đại từ và cấu trúc cố định.

questions.prompt: Blank 1

This approach makes the writer sound fair-minded rather (1) _____ defensive. However, the rebuttal must be specific. It is not enough to say that the other side is wrong; the writer must show (2) _____ its reasoning is incomplete. Done well, concession and rebuttal can make an argument much (3) _____ powerful.

problems.title: Error Identification Set 01
problems.statement: Xác định lỗi sai trong câu và viết lại phần đúng.
problems.instructions: Chọn phần/lỗi sai và đưa ra cách sửa. Một số câu có thể là bẫy không lỗi nếu được đánh dấu trong đáp án.

questions.prompt: Despite of the heavy rain, the debate continued as planned.
questions.explanation: Despite is not followed by of.

metadata.sentence: Despite of the heavy rain, the debate continued as planned.

questions.prompt: The number of students taking the exam have increased sharply.
questions.explanation: The subject is The number, singular.

metadata.sentence: The number of students taking the exam have increased sharply.

questions.prompt: Hardly the teacher had entered the room when the class fell silent.

answer.display: A: Hardly had the teacher entered

metadata.sentence: Hardly the teacher had entered the room when the class fell silent.

questions.prompt: She suggested me to rewrite the introduction more clearly.

answer.correction: suggested that I rewrite / suggested rewriting
answer.display: A: suggested that I rewrite / suggested rewriting

questions.explanation: suggest does not take object + to-infinitive.

metadata.sentence: She suggested me to rewrite the introduction more clearly.
metadata.correction: suggested that I rewrite / suggested rewriting

questions.prompt: The essay is consisted of four main sections.

metadata.sentence: The essay is consisted of four main sections.

problems.title: Error Identification Set 02
problems.statement: Xác định lỗi sai trong câu và viết lại phần đúng.
problems.instructions: Chọn phần/lỗi sai và đưa ra cách sửa. Một số câu có thể là bẫy không lỗi nếu được đánh dấu trong đáp án.

questions.prompt: Neither the students nor the teacher were aware of the change.
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with nearer subject teacher.

metadata.sentence: Neither the students nor the teacher were aware of the change.

questions.prompt: It is essential that every applicant submits the form on time.

metadata.sentence: It is essential that every applicant submits the form on time.

questions.prompt: The explanation was too complicated that most students gave up.

answer.correction: so complicated that / too complicated for
answer.display: C: so complicated that / too complicated for

metadata.sentence: The explanation was too complicated that most students gave up.
metadata.correction: so complicated that / too complicated for

questions.prompt: She is one of the most capable student in the class.

metadata.sentence: She is one of the most capable student in the class.

questions.prompt: I look forward to hear from you soon.

metadata.sentence: I look forward to hear from you soon.

problems.title: Error Identification Set 03
problems.statement: Xác định lỗi sai trong câu và viết lại phần đúng.
problems.instructions: Chọn phần/lỗi sai và đưa ra cách sửa. Một số câu có thể là bẫy không lỗi nếu được đánh dấu trong đáp án.

questions.prompt: The reason why he failed was because he ignored the instructions.

metadata.sentence: The reason why he failed was because he ignored the instructions.

questions.prompt: No matter how hard did he try, he could not solve the puzzle.
questions.explanation: No matter how does not use inversion here.

metadata.sentence: No matter how hard did he try, he could not solve the puzzle.

questions.prompt: The data shows that reading habits has changed.

metadata.sentence: The data shows that reading habits has changed.

questions.prompt: Scarcely had we sat down than the fire alarm rang.

metadata.sentence: Scarcely had we sat down than the fire alarm rang.

questions.prompt: Each of the answers were checked twice.

metadata.sentence: Each of the answers were checked twice.

problems.title: Error Identification Set 04
problems.statement: Xác định lỗi sai trong câu và viết lại phần đúng.
problems.instructions: Chọn phần/lỗi sai và đưa ra cách sửa. Một số câu có thể là bẫy không lỗi nếu được đánh dấu trong đáp án.

questions.prompt: Not only he passed the exam, but he also won a scholarship.

questions.prompt: Had I knew the answer, I would have told you.

problems.title: Trios / Gapped Sentences Set 01
problems.statement: Mỗi câu hỏi gồm ba câu có cùng một từ bị khuyết.
problems.instructions: Điền một từ phù hợp với cả ba câu. Chú ý collocation và nghĩa khác nhau của cùng một từ.

questions.prompt: Điền một từ duy nhất phù hợp với cả ba câu.
questions.passage:
1. The committee will _____ the proposal next week.
2. You should _____ all the evidence before reaching a conclusion.
3. I would never _____ such an irresponsible suggestion.

sentences: The committee will _____ the proposal next week.
You should _____ all the evidence before reaching a conclusion.
I would never _____ such an irresponsible suggestion.

questions.prompt: Điền một từ duy nhất phù hợp với cả ba câu.
questions.passage:
1. The policy was introduced to _____ economic growth.

3. The article helped _____ a lively debate.

A good teacher knows how to _____ curiosity.
The article helped _____ a lively debate.

questions.prompt: Điền một từ duy nhất phù hợp với cả ba câu.

questions.prompt: Điền một từ duy nhất phù hợp với cả ba câu.

problems.title: Trios / Gapped Sentences Set 02

problems.title: Reading MCQ: Why difficulty helps learning
problems.statement: Đọc đoạn văn và trả lời câu hỏi.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào main idea, inference, vocabulary in context, detail và tone.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
questions.passage: Students often prefer exercises that feel smooth and familiar. Yet classroom practice suggests that a certain amount of difficulty can make learning stronger. When a task is slightly beyond a learner comfort zone, the brain has to retrieve, compare and adjust information rather than merely recognise it. This effort may feel inefficient at first, but it often leads to longer-lasting understanding. The key word, however, is slightly. If a task is far too hard, students may stop trying and simply guess. Good practice therefore creates productive struggle: enough challenge to demand thought, but enough support to make success possible.

options.label: A
options.text: Easy tasks are always better for confidence.

options.label: C
options.text: Students should avoid guessing in exams.

options.label: D
options.text: Teachers should make every task extremely hard.

questions.explanation: The passage argues for productive struggle.

questions.prompt: The word retrieve is closest in meaning to:

When a task is slightly beyond a learner comfort zone, the brain has to retrieve, compare and adjust information rather than merely recognise it. Good practice therefore creates productive struggle: enough challenge to demand thought, but enough support to make success possible.

If a task is far too hard, students may stop trying and simply guess. questions.explanation: The text says far too hard tasks may make students stop trying and guess.

questions.explanation: The writer acknowledges both benefits and limits.

problems.title: Reading MCQ: The problem with perfect notes
problems.statement: Đọc đoạn văn và trả lời câu hỏi.
problems.instructions: Tập trung vào main idea, inference, vocabulary in context, detail và tone.

questions.prompt: What is the writer main warning?
questions.passage: Beautiful notes can give students a sense of control, but they do not always lead to better learning. Copying explanations neatly is different from being able to use them. A learner may spend an hour highlighting rules about inversion and still fail to notice an inversion trigger in a sentence transformation task. This does not mean notes are useless. They are valuable when they become tools for active recall: covering examples, predicting answers, explaining traps, and rewriting rules in one own words. The problem begins when the appearance of learning replaces the act of thinking.

options.label: B
options.text: Neat notes can create a false sense of learning.

options.label: C
options.text: Highlighting is better than active recall.

options.label: D
options.text: Inversion is the hardest grammar topic.

questions.explanation: The passage warns against appearance replacing thinking.

questions.prompt: According to the passage, notes are useful when they:
questions.passage: Beautiful notes can give students a sense of control, but they do not always lead to better learning. Copying explanations neatly is different from being able to use them. A learner may spend an hour highlighting rules about inversion and still fail to notice an inversion trigger in a sentence transformation task. This does not mean notes are useless. They are valuable when they become tools for active recall: covering examples, predicting answers, explaining traps, and rewriting rules in one own words. The problem begins when the appearance of learning replaces the act of thinking.

options.label: A
options.text: are copied from the teacher exactly

questions.explanation: Notes become tools for active recall.

questions.prompt: The phrase appearance of learning refers to:
questions.passage: Beautiful notes can give students a sense of control, but they do not always lead to better learning. Copying explanations neatly is different from being able to use them. A learner may spend an hour highlighting rules about inversion and still fail to notice an inversion trigger in a sentence transformation task. This does not mean notes are useless. They are valuable when they become tools for active recall: covering examples, predicting answers, explaining traps, and rewriting rules in one own words. The problem begins when the appearance of learning replaces the act of thinking.

options.label: A
options.text: looking like one is studying without deep processing

questions.explanation: It means surface-level activity that feels like learning.
